headline: MACC's unexplained wealth orders used only 4 times in 5 years
context: MACC's power to issue unexplained wealth orders under the 2019 amendment has been used only four times in five years, despite being designed as a key anti-corruption tool.

cards.big: Unexplained wealth orders used just 4 times in 5 years — powerful tool gathering dust
cards.sub: Law designed to target wealth that cannot be legally explained sits largely unused

cards.big: MACC Act Section 36A allows civil forfeiture where wealth exceeds known income
cards.sub: Lower burden of proof than criminal prosecution — requires only balance of probabilities

cards.big: UK's National Crime Agency used unexplained wealth orders 28 times in same period
cards.sub: Comparable jurisdictions deploy the tool far more aggressively against suspect wealth

cards.big: MACC's wealth declaration database covers only 300,000 of 1.7 million civil servants
cards.sub: Incomplete data makes it difficult to identify unexplained wealth systematically

cards.big: Having a powerful tool and not using it is worse than not having it — it signals unwillingness
Why is MACC reluctant to use its most potent anti-corruption weapon?
